title: Sub-lemma 2b: telescoping sum and equivalence {sum_S z >= 0} = {Y >= p}

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Obligations (the hinge identities of Claim 2).**
1. **Zero-sum.** $\sum_{i=1}^n y_i=\langle w,\sum_{i=1}^n r_i\rangle=\langle w,\mathbf 1\rangle=\sum_{j=1}^m w_j=1$, using that each of the $m$ columns contributes its single $1$ to exactly one row. Hence $\sum_{i=1}^n z_i = 1 - n\cdot\tfrac1n = 0$, supplying the MMS hypothesis.
2. **Threshold equivalence.** For any $S$ with $|S|=k$:
$$\sum_{i\in S} z_i\ge 0 \iff \sum_{i\in S} y_i\ge \tfrac{|S|}{n}=\tfrac{k}{n}=p \iff Y\ge p.$$
These are short but must be written explicitly, as the whole reduction passes through them.
